# Probe the cmd2mcpserver tool
# This test uses mcp-probe to probe the cmd2mcpserver to test its MCP server functionality

[!unix] skip 'This test requires Unix-like environment'

# First, use cmd2mcpserver to convert itself to an MCP server
exec cmd2mcpserver ${MCPSCRIPTTEST_DIR}
cmp stdout mcpserver/main.go

# Now that we have the MCP server version, let's build it
cd mcpserver
exec go build -o cmd2mcpserver-mcp

# Use mcp-probe to test the generated server
exec mcp-probe ./cmd2mcpserver-mcp

# The mcp-probe output should show the server capabilities
stdout 'Probe Results for'
stdout 'SERVER INITIALIZATION'
stdout 'tools/list'

-- expected_main.go --
// Code generated by cmd2mcpserver. DO NOT EDIT.

package main

import (
	"bufio"
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"

	"github.com/tmc/mcp"
	"github.com/tmc/mcp/modelcontextprotocol"
)

var (
	verbose  = flag.Bool("verbose", false, "enable verbose output")
	dryRun   = flag.Bool("dry-run", false, "Output generated source as txtar to stdout")
	probeCmd = flag.String("probe", "", "Command to probe (e.g., 'go run ./cmd/myserver')")
	debug    = flag.Bool("debug", false, "show debug output")
)

func main() {
	flag.Parse()

	srv := mcpToolServer()
	srv.Debug = *debug

	if *verbose {
		srv.Verbose = true
		log.Println("Starting cmd2mcpserver MCP server...")
	}

	s := mcp.NewServer(srv)
	if err := s.ServeStdio(); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}

func mcpToolServer() mcp.ServerTools {
	return mcp.ServerTools{
		Info: &modelcontextprotocol.ImplementationInfo{
			Name:    "cmd2mcpserver",
			Version: "0.1.0",
		},
		Tools: []modelcontextprotocol.Tool{
			{
				Name:        "cmd2mcpserver",
				Description: "cmd2mcpserver converts existing binaries into Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers.\n\nIt automatically extracts flags from applications using github.com/tmc/mcp/exp/sourcereflect\nand wraps the binary execution behind a JSON-RPC interface.\n\nUsage:\n  cmd2mcpserver [flags] /path/to/binary/or/module\n",
				InputSchema: json.RawMessage(`{"type":"object","properties":{"debug":{"type":"boolean","description":"show debug output"},"dry-run":{"type":"boolean","description":"Output generated source as txtar to stdout"},"probe":{"type":"string","description":"Command to probe (e.g., 'go run ./cmd/myserver')"},"verbose":{"type":"boolean","description":"enable verbose output"}},"required":[]}`),
			},
		},
		ToolCallHandler: runToolHandler(),
	}
}

func runToolHandler() func(context.Context, modelcontextprotocol.ToolCallRequest) (modelcontextprotocol.ToolCallResult, error) {
	return func(ctx context.Context, req modelcontextprotocol.ToolCallRequest) (modelcontextprotocol.ToolCallResult, error) {
		switch req.Name {
		case "cmd2mcpserver":
			return runCmd2mcpserver(ctx, req.Arguments)
		default:
			return modelcontextprotocol.ToolCallResult{}, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool: %s", req.Name)
		}
	}
}

func runCmd2mcpserver(ctx context.Context, args json.RawMessage) (modelcontextprotocol.ToolCallResult, error) {
	// Parse the arguments
	var input struct {
		Debug    bool   `json:"debug"`
		DryRun   bool   `json:"dry-run"`
		Probe    string `json:"probe"`
		Verbose  bool   `json:"verbose"`
	}
	if err := json.Unmarshal(args, &input); err != nil {
		return modelcontextprotocol.ToolCallResult{}, fmt.Errorf("invalid arguments: %w", err)
	}

	// Prepare command arguments
	cmdArgs := []string{}
	if input.Debug {
		cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs, "-debug")
	}
	if input.DryRun {
		cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs, "-dry-run")
	}
	if input.Probe != "" {
		cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs, "-probe", input.Probe)
	}
	if input.Verbose {
		cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs, "-verbose")
	}

	// Execute the original command
	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "./cmd2mcpserver", cmdArgs...)
	
	var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
	cmd.Stderr = &stderr

	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
		return modelcontextprotocol.ToolCallResult{
			Failure: &modelcontextprotocol.FailureResult{
				Kind:        modelcontextprotocol.FailureResultKindDirect,
				Description: err.Error(),
				Metadata:    &map[string]interface{}{"stderr": stderr.String()},
			},
		}, nil
	}

	return modelcontextprotocol.ToolCallResult{
		Success: &modelcontextprotocol.SuccessResult{
			Content: json.RawMessage(fmt.Sprintf(`{"stdout":%q,"stderr":%q}`, stdout.String(), stderr.String())),
		},
	}, nil
}
